humanities_role_explanation: Designed specifically for linguistic understanding of idiomatic expressions, a core phenomenon in language and linguistics research
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Multi-modal benchmark combining text (context sentences with idiomatic expressions), static images (depicting literal/figurative meanings), and temporal image sequences (three-panel comic-style progressions)
analytical_tasks_explanation: Task A requires ranking images based on alignment with idiomatic vs literal meanings in context. Task B involves predicting narrative completion in image sequences while distinguishing literal from figurative interpretations. Both require deep semantic understanding of non-compositional language phenomena.

general_purpose_benchmark.note: Evaluates general capability of models to understand idiomatic expressions across multiple languages and modalities

evaluates_generative_llm.note: GPT-4, QWEN, LLaVA, CLIP, RoBERTa, SBERT, DeepSeek, and other large language/vision models were evaluated

llm_is_primary_system.note: LLMs and VLMs are the primary systems being benchmarked for idiomaticity understanding

arts_humanities_data.note: Language and Linguistics - focuses on idiomatic expressions, multi-word expressions, and figurative language processing

quantitative_metrics.note: Top Image Accuracy, Discounted Cumulative Gain (DCG), Image Completion Accuracy, Sentence Type Accuracy

english_language.note: Paper is in English, benchmark includes both English and Brazilian Portuguese data

summary: AdMIRe introduces a multimodal benchmark for evaluating models' understanding of idiomatic expressions through image ranking and sequence completion tasks. The dataset contains 100 English and 55 Portuguese potentially idiomatic noun compounds with corresponding visual representations depicting both literal and figurative meanings.
key_findings: Top-performing systems achieved human-level performance (0.93 top-1 accuracy) using mixture-of-experts approaches with multiple LLM queries. However, this required substantial computational resources and prompt engineering. Models exhibited bias toward idiomatic interpretations regardless of context.
